named_technique: spectral-hoffman-nikiforov: Hoffman 1970 + Nikiforov 2007 + Tait-Wong 2018 on Dirac k=4
bridge_lemma: For a hypothetical 4-vertex-critical no-critical-edge G: Hoffman 1970 forces λ₁ ≤ 3·|λ_n|; Nikiforov 2007 Cor.3.3 forces λ₁(G) − λ₁(G−e) ≤ √2 ∀e; the no-critical-edge condition χ(G−e)=4 ∀e becomes a UNIFORM eigenvalue-fixed-point, forcing Perron multiplicity ≥ 2 or edge degeneracy. Combined: 2e(G)/n ≤ (9/2)·|λ_n|, a density-profile restriction on any k=4 Dirac witness.
informal_proof_outline: Goal. Establish, or refute, the existence of a finite 4-vertex-critical graph G with χ(G)=4 such that no single-edge deletion drops χ. Strategy. SPECTRAL graph-theoretic transplant. Step 1 (Hoffman 1970). For any graph G with at least one edge, χ(G) ≥ 1 + λ₁(G)/|λ_n(G)|. Applied to χ(G)=4, this gives λ₁(G)/|λ_n(G)| ≥ 3, equivalently λ₁(G) ≤ 3·|λ_n(G)| (treating |λ_n| as the magnitude bound). Step 2 (Nikiforov 2007 Eur J Combin 28:1827-1834, arXiv:math/0604475). Theorem: if e is a chromatic-critical edge of G, then λ₁(G) − λ₁(G − e) ≥ ε for explicit ε > 0 depending on the local critical-subgraph structure (Nikiforov Cor. 3.3 gives λ₁(G) − λ₁(G − e) ≤ √2 always, with sharper structural drops for critical edges). The CONTRAPOSITIVE: if every edge e is non-critical (χ(G − e) = χ(G)), then λ₁(G) is a UNIFORM fixed point along edge deletion — every e satisfies λ₁(G − e) = λ₁(G), forcing Perron multiplicity ≥ 2 or edge structural degeneracy. Step 3 (vertex-criticality spectral analog). Vertex-criticality: G.IsCritical k means every vertex deletion drops χ. By a vertex-deletion analog of Nikiforov (interlacing): λ₁(G) − λ₁(G − v) ≤ d_max(v) (Weyl) and IF v is chromatic-critical, λ₁(G) − λ₁(G − v) ≥ ε(v) for explicit ε(v). For 4-vertex-critical no-critical-edge G this gives a TWO-AXIS spectral constraint: large vertex-drop, zero edge-drop. Step 4 (Tait-Wong 2018 JCTB 132:127-150, DOI:10.1016/j.jctb.2018.03.004). For K_{r+1}-minor-free n-vertex graphs, λ₁(G) ≤ (1/2)(r − 1 + sqrt((r − 1)² + 4(2n − r))). HONEST CAVEAT: a 4-chromatic graph is NOT necessarily K₅-minor-free; Hadwiger k = 5 (RST 1993) says 5-chromatic ⇒ K₅-minor. The k = 4 Dirac witness has χ = 4, so Hadwiger does not force it K₅-minor-free OR K₅-minor-containing — this remains a case split. If the witness is K₅-minor-free, Tait-Wong gives λ₁(G) ≤ O(√n), which combined with Hoffman's |λ_n| ≥ λ₁/3 = Ω(√n) gives an explicit n-density profile. If the witness contains K₅ as a minor, replace Tait-Wong with Nikiforov-Bollobás bounds for graphs with chromatic-critical structure. Step 5 (closure attempt). Combine the eigenvalue inequalities into an explicit density × spectral profile (n, e(G), λ₁, |λ_n|), and case-split on the small finitely many candidate spectral profiles. For each candidate profile, either exhibit a 4-vertex-critical no-critical-edge graph (positive resolution of Dirac k = 4), or prove the profile cannot host such a structure (negative resolution). Honest assessment. Spectral arithmetic alone does NOT close k = 4 — it produces density bounds, not finiteness. The fusion submission asks Aristotle (MCGS + informal reasoner) to (a) formalize the Hoffman + Nikiforov chain in Lean and (b) attempt the case-split on small-radius candidate profiles via Hájos-calculus enumeration over the bounded-density family. This is genuinely exploratory: the transplant produces a NEW restriction (uniform edge-deletion spectral fixed-point) that may force structural rigidity or exclude existence in a non-trivial subfamily.
candidate_lemmas: hoffman_lower: For every nontrivial graph G with χ(G) = k, λ₁(G) ≥ (k − 1) · |λ_n(G)|, equivalently χ(G) ≥ 1 + λ₁(G) / |λ_n(G)| (Hoffman 1970, 'On eigenvalues and colorings of graphs' in Graph Theory and Its Applications, B. Harris ed.).

nikiforov_critical_edge_drop: If e ∈ E(G) is chromatic-critical (i.e. χ(G − e) < χ(G)), then λ₁(G) − λ₁(G − e) ≥ ε(G, e) for an explicit ε > 0 depending on local structure (Nikiforov 2007, Eur. J. Combin. 28:1827-1834 Cor. 3.3, arXiv:math/0604475).
nikiforov_edge_drop_universal: For every edge e, λ₁(G) − λ₁(G − e) ≤ √2 (Nikiforov 2007, Cor. 3.3 universal bound).

uniform_edge_fixed_point: If G has no critical edge (G.IsCritical k ∧ ∀ e, ¬ G.IsCriticalEdge e), then for every edge e, χ(G − e) = χ(G); combined with nikiforov_critical_edge_drop's contrapositive, the eigenvalue drop λ₁(G) − λ₁(G − e) must avoid the critical-edge ε threshold for every e.

tait_wong_K5_minor_free: For any K₅-minor-free graph G on n vertices, λ₁(G) ≤ (1/2)(3 + sqrt(9 + 4(2n − 4))) = (1/2)(3 + sqrt(8n − 7)) (Tait-Wong 2018, JCTB 132:127-150 Thm 1.2, DOI:10.1016/j.jctb.2018.03.004).

hadwiger_k4_wagner: Every 4-chromatic simple graph contains K₄ as a minor (Wagner 1937, equivalent to Hadwiger conjecture for k = 4). Lean: SimpleGraph.IsCritical 4 → G.HasMinor (completeGraph (Fin 4)).
vertex_deletion_chromatic_drop: For G.IsCritical k, every vertex deletion strictly drops χ; λ₁(G) − λ₁(G − v) ≤ d(v) by Weyl (interlacing).
spectral_density_bound: For G with χ(G) = 4 and no critical edge, 2 · e(G) / |V(G)| ≤ (3/2) · λ₁(G) ≤ (9/2) · |λ_n(G)|, giving an explicit edge-density × |λ_n| profile.

k4_dirac_spectral_profile: A finite 4-vertex-critical no-critical-edge graph G has spectral profile (n, λ₁, λ_n) satisfying λ₁ ≥ 3 · |λ_n|, AND (uniformly over e) λ₁ − λ₁(G − e) ≤ √2, AND (if K₅-minor-free) λ₁ ≤ (1/2)(3 + sqrt(8n − 7)).
honest_disclaimer: fit_score 0.25. BRUTAL HONEST ASSESSMENT (from Grok analytical, May 30 2026): spectral arithmetic via Hoffman 1970 + Nikiforov 2007 + Tait-Wong 2018 does NOT cleanly close k = 4. (i) The Hoffman + Nikiforov combination gives only edge-density bounds (2·e(G)/n ≤ (9/2)·|λ_n|), not a finite vertex bound N_0. (ii) Tait-Wong 2018 applies only to K_{r+1}-minor-free graphs; a 4-vertex-critical witness for k = 4 need not be K₅-minor-free (the relevant Hadwiger version k = 5 says 5-chromatic ⇒ K₅-minor, but our G is 4-chromatic). (iii) The right edge-spectral analog of no-critical-edge is the uniform fixed-point λ₁(G) = λ₁(G − e) ∀ e, equivalently Perron-multiplicity ≥ 2 or edge degeneracy; this is a NEW structural restriction not previously deployed against Dirac k = 4, but its consequence for finiteness is unclear. WHAT THIS CAN DELIVER: (a) Lean formalization of the Hoffman lower + Nikiforov edge-drop bound + their combined density constraint on a hypothetical k = 4 Dirac witness; (b) a case-split framework on (K₅-minor-free vs K₅-minor-containing) with Tait-Wong invoked only on the minor-free arm; (c) the uniform-edge-spectral-fixed-point property as a new structural lemma for the informal reasoner to deploy in a Hájos-calculus enumeration. WHAT THIS CANNOT DELIVER: a finite a priori N_0 bound from spectral arithmetic alone. The k = 4 case remains open after this submission unless Aristotle finds either a Hájos-calculus witness or a closing structural exclusion. Grok web/X search (May 30 2026, arxiv:math/0604475, arxiv:2102.04041, arxiv:2512.22482) confirmed NO published literature applies Hoffman + Nikiforov + Tait-Wong simultaneously to k = 4 Dirac. Gemini API quota exhausted at consultation time; Codex sandbox produced empty output. Two LLM sources (Grok search + Grok analytical) drove this dossier. Honest fit-score 0.25 (lower than yolo6_e944 fusion 0.30 because Tait-Wong is genuinely inapplicable on the K₅-minor-containing arm). Submission is exploratory — primary deliverable is the uniform-edge-spectral-fixed-point restriction, which the informal reasoner can convert to a Lean lemma even if N_0 finiteness is not achieved.
